name: Gorges of Les Planes d'Hostoles Loop
name_original: Els gorgs de Planes d'Hostoles - Circular por el Espacio Natural Protegido del río Brugent
description: Hike a moderate 8.1-mile (13.1 km) circular route through the Brugent Protected Natural Area, exploring stunning natural pools and waterfall
region_intro: Hiking around Les Planes D'Hostoles offers diverse natural landscapes, characterized by rivers, streams, small valleys, and steep cliffs. The region is adjacent to the Garrotxa Volcanic Zone Natural Park, providing a unique geological dimension to the trails. Numerous gorges and natural pools, formed by the Brugent River, are prominent features, especially within the Brugent Protected Natural Area. This area provides varied terrain suitable for different hiking experiences. Best hiking trails around Les Planes D'Hostoles The most popular hiking route is Gorges of Les Planes d'Hostoles Loop, a 8.1 miles (13.1 km) trail that takes 3 hours 31 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the natural gorges and pools of the Brugent Protected Natural Area. Another top favourite among local hikers is Molí dels Murris Pool – Duran Gorge loop from les Planes d'Hostoles, a moderate 5.9 miles (9.5 km) path. This trail leads through riverine environments, showcasing natural pools and gorges. Local hikers also love the Santuari de la Salut Viewpoint – Enchanted Rocks loop from Reserva natural parcial del volcà de Sant Marc, a 3.2 miles (5.2 km) trail leading through historical sites and unique rock formations, often completed in about 1 hour 29 minutes. Hiking around Les Planes D'Hostoles is defined by gorges, natural pools, and volcanic landscapes. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult trails, accommodating various ability levels. The routes in Les Planes D'Hostoles are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from more than 350 reviews. More than 2200 hikers have used komoot to explore Les Planes D'Hostoles's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are there in Les Planes D'Hostoles?
A: Les Planes D'Hostoles offers a diverse network of over 35 hiking trails. These routes vary in difficulty, providing options for easy strolls, moderate treks, and more challenging adventures through the region's unique landscapes.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for families or beginners?
A: Yes, Les Planes D'Hostoles has several easy trails perfect for families or those seeking a relaxed walk. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31851335" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santuari de la Salut Viewpoint – Enchanted Rocks loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is 3.2 miles (5.2 km) long and takes you through historical sites and unique rock formations. Another gentle route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14961049" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Margarida Pool loop&lt;/a&gt;, a 3.5 km trail that explores one of the area's beautiful natural pools.

Q: Where can I find trails with waterfalls or natural pools?
A: The region is renowned for its stunning gorges and natural pools, primarily formed by the Brugent River. You can explore routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31878004" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riera de Cogolls Waterfall – Molí dels Murris Pool loop&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you past impressive cascades and swimming spots. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4391819" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Molí dels Murris Pool&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4391823" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Plana Pool&lt;/a&gt; are particularly popular for their beauty and are often included in local hiking loops.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Les Planes D'Hostoles?
A: Many of the trails in Les Planes D'Hostoles are designed as circular routes, allowing you to start and end at the same point. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e938193675/gorges-of-les-planes-d-hostoles-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorges of Les Planes d'Hostoles Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 8.1-mile (13.1 km) circular trail that explores the natural gorges and pools of the Brugent Protected Natural Area.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the hiking trails?
A: The terrain around Les Planes D'Hostoles is highly varied. You'll encounter lush riverine environments, small valleys, and steep cliffs. The area's proximity to the Garrotxa Volcanic Zone Natural Park also means you might find landscapes shaped by ancient volcanic activity, adding a distinct geological dimension to your hike.

Q: What are some interesting landmarks or attractions to see along the trails?
A: Beyond the natural beauty, trails often lead to historical and cultural sites. You can discover the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/683536" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rupit Village&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1134337" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Margarida Volcano&lt;/a&gt;, or the intriguing &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31851335" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roques Encantades (Enchanted Rocks)&lt;/a&gt;. The remains of historical castles like Hostoles Castle are also part of the region's rich heritage.

Q: Is Les Planes D'Hostoles a good destination for dog-friendly hikes?
A: Les Planes D'Hostoles is generally welcoming for dog owners, with many trails passing through natural areas. However, it's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially in protected areas like the Brugent Protected Natural Area, to protect local wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all hikers. Specific rules may apply near natural pools or sensitive ecological zones.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Les Planes D'Hostoles?
A: The spring and autumn months are generally ideal for hiking in Les Planes D'Hostoles. In spring, the waterfalls and natural pools are particularly vibrant due to increased water flow, and the temperatures are mild. Autumn offers pleasant weather and beautiful fall foliage. Summers can be warm, making early morning or late afternoon hikes preferable, especially if you plan to enjoy the natural pools.

Q: Are there any challenging trails for experienced hikers?
A: Yes, for experienced hikers seeking a challenge, Les Planes D'Hostoles offers several difficult routes. These trails often feature steeper ascents and descents, covering more rugged terrain. While specific challenging routes are not listed here, the region's diverse landscape, including mountain ranges and steep cliffs, provides ample opportunity for more demanding treks.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Les Planes D'Hostoles?
A: The trails in Les Planes D'Hostoles are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 350 reviews. Hikers often praise the stunning natural pools and gorges, the varied terrain that includes volcanic landscapes, and the well-maintained paths that cater to different ability levels.

Q: Is there public transport access to the hiking trails?
A: While Les Planes D'Hostoles is accessible by road, public transport options directly to trailheads might be limited. It's advisable to check local bus schedules or consider driving to the starting points of your chosen hikes. Many routes begin near the town, offering easier access.

Q: Where can I find parking for hiking routes?
A: Parking is generally available near the main trailheads and within the town of Les Planes D'Hostoles. For popular spots like the gorges and natural pools, designated parking areas are often provided. It's recommended to arrive early during peak season to secure a spot, especially for routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10483642" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Molí dels Murris Pool – Duran Gorge loop&lt;/a&gt;.
tips: This beautiful natural water pool formed by two waterfalls is the easiest to access of the Planes d'Hostoles pools. It is an ideal route to go with the whole family.

This waterfall is just five minutes from the Gorg de la Plana. If you visit the first, you have to visit the second, which is a little more spectacular. You need a reservation to visit. More information here: espaiprotegitdelbrugent.cat/es.
One of the natural pools along the Brugent River. It is located in the municipality of Plains d'Hostoles

The waters of the Cogolls stream are filtered and become groundwater in the area. They are enriched with dissolved calcium bicarbonate, which petrifies the plants and shapes the travertines of the waterfall. It is one of the most beautiful pools in the Planes d'Hostoles.

Natural pool located in the municipality of Planes d'Hostoles. It is in the course of the Brugent River and when it carries enough water, a waterfall of considerable dimensions is formed that makes this place a spectacular site.
Its access is from the El Carrilet Greenway from Girona to Olot.

faqs.question: How long does it take to hike the gorgs of Planes d'Hostoles circular route?
faqs.answer: This circular route typically takes around 3 hours and 30 minutes to complete. It covers a distance of approximately 13 kilometers.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail features diverse surfaces, including paved sections, unpaved paths, and natural footpaths. You'll be walking alongside the Brugent River, enjoying the stunning natural pools and waterfalls.

faqs.question: What natural features can I see along the way?
faqs.answer: The primary attractions are the beautiful natural pools (gorgs) and waterfalls formed by the Brugent River. Key highlights include the La Plana Pool, Duran Gorge, La Laura Pool and the Gran de Can Paulí Weir, Molí dels Murris Pool, and Santa Margarida Pool. The Molí dels Murris is particularly known for its unique petrifying waters.

faqs.question: How difficult is this hike, and is it suitable for families?
faqs.answer: The trail is generally considered moderate. While many parts are flat and accessible, some sections may require good fitness and sure-footedness. The Gorg de la Plana is highlighted as an ideal and easily accessible spot for families.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the gorgs trail?
faqs.answer: Designated parking areas are available in Les Planes d'Hostoles. You can find parking in the town center or near the cemetery, which serve as convenient starting points for exploring the different gorgs.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to visit the gorgs?
faqs.answer: Spring is ideal when the river carries ample water, creating spectacular cascades. To avoid crowds and restrictions, consider visiting outside the peak summer season (typically June 24 to September 11). If visiting in summer, arrive early in the morning.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or reservation to visit the gorgs?
faqs.answer: During the peak summer season (typically June 24 to September 11), access to the gorgs is restricted and requires prior reservations. Outside of this period, there may be no entrance fee or fewer restrictions.

faqs.question: Is the trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, dogs are allowed on the trail, but they must be kept on a leash at all times to protect the natural environment and local wildlife.

faqs.question: Are there any regulations I should be aware of in the Protected Natural Space of the Brugent River?
faqs.answer: Yes, as part of a protected area, specific regulations are in place. These include prohibiting eating, camping, or making fires, requiring dogs to be on a leash, and emphasizing the preservation of local flora and fauna. Please respect these rules to help maintain the natural beauty of the area.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route incorporates sections of several other paths, including the PirineXus, the Ruta del Carrilet I: "Girona-Olot", and the Via Verda Carrilet Olot - Girona. It also overlaps with parts of the Camí del nord o del Canigó and the Volta a peu a la Garrotxa.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or stay near the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the town of Les Planes d'Hostoles offers amenities. A local restaurant, Bar Restaurant La Caseta, is mentioned as a good option for food near one of the parking areas.

waypoints.name: La Laura Pool and the Gran de Can Paulí Weir
